title: gerardia acuta
intro: Gerardia acuta, also known as the narrow-leaved gerardia or American foxglove, is a perennial plant native to North America. The plant's tubular flowers have medicinal properties, particularly its leaves and roots, which contain cardenolides that have been used to treat various health conditions. Gerardia acuta has been traditionally used to treat fever, rheumatism, and as a diuretic, and its health benefits also include lowering blood pressure and reducing inflammation. Herbal preparations of Gerardia acuta include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures that can be used internally or applied topically, but caution should be exercised due to potential toxicity.
